office_does: The council adopts city laws, budgets, taxes, development policy, and service priorities and oversees the city manager.
race_blurb: Five candidates contest a three-year unexpired term, contrasting fiscal restraint, growth management, public participation, infrastructure, environmental concerns, and administrative experience.

candidates.summary: Tveraas is a project and IT manager who emphasizes public review of major industrial proposals, community planning, and transparent decisions.
highlights: Calls for public review of proposed data-center and fertilizer-plant impacts [S1]
Supports community-driven planning, accountability, public safety, and transparent decisions [S1]

spending.basis: He foregrounds public scrutiny and accountability for major city decisions.

experience.basis: He has substantial project-management experience but presents as a new voice in elected city government.

candidates.summary: Saltz is an Army veteran and VFW leader running on lower taxes, budget restraint, public safety, and reducing municipal bureaucracy.
highlights: Supports lower taxes, budget surpluses, and responsible spending [S1]
Calls for safe neighborhoods and removal of outdated codes and bureaucracy [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly campaigns for lower taxes.

spending.basis: He calls for budget surpluses, responsible spending, and less bureaucracy.

safety.basis: Safe neighborhoods are a stated priority, though no enforcement mechanism is specified.

experience.basis: His military, Hanford, and VFW leadership are relevant but outside elected city government.

candidates.summary: Lipton is a scientist, professor, and small-business owner who supports managed growth, infrastructure, environmental protection, and collaborative fiscal stewardship.
highlights: Supports managed growth, infrastructure, workforce development, parks, and river access [S1]
Emphasizes environmental protection, fiscal stewardship, transparency, and collaboration [S1]

climate.basis: She identifies environmental protection as a city priority, without specifying a policy mechanism.

experience.basis: She brings scientific, business, and civic experience but no stated elected-city record.

candidates.summary: Faylor is an Army veteran and real-estate appraiser running on independent representation and opposition to preferential deals.
highlights: Opposes deals that benefit only well-connected interests [S1]

experience.basis: Her professional and volunteer background is outside elected municipal office and is presented as an independent perspective.

candidates.summary: Samuel reports current council service after planning and code-enforcement roles and emphasizes infrastructure, efficient government, and responsible development.
highlights: Prioritizes infrastructure maintenance, efficient government, and responsible development [S1]

spending.basis: Efficient government and data-driven administration are central to his stated agenda.

experience.basis: He reports current council service plus prior planning, code-enforcement, and project-management work.
